contest.title: 2025-2026 ICPC, NERC, Northern Eurasia Finals (Unrated, Online Mirror, ICPC Rules, Teams Preferred)

sources.notes: Official NERC tutorial; used to confirm the cactus cycle fact, 16-bracket maintenance, monotone connectivity predicate, and second pass for bridge/cycle length classification.

reference_candidates.notes: 洛谷题解候选来源；只有实际参考后才移动到 sources。

annotation.summary: Interactive classification of every edge in a cactus-like graph using connectivity queries on chosen edge subsets.
annotation.constraints: The query budget is tight, so each edge needs only a constant number of connectivity queries.
annotation.core_idea: Use the cactus fact that deleting two distinct edges from the same simple cycle disconnects the graph. Process edges by label and place them into one of 16 brackets: bracket i stores non-bridge edges that have exactly i earlier edges on their unique cycle, and bracket 15 stores bridges or edges deep in long cycles. For the current edge e, maintain base graphs G_i obtained by deleting already-placed bracket-i edges; each G_i remains connected. The predicate Ask(G_i \ {e}) is monotone in i, so a 4-query binary search finds e's bracket. A second pass uses the same bracket predicates to distinguish bridges, big cycles, and exact cycle length up to 15.
annotation.complexity: O(m log 16) connectivity queries, i.e. constant queries per edge.

solution_variants.name: main
solution_variants.summary: Maintain 16 connected base graphs, binary-search the bracket of each edge with connectivity queries, then classify bridge/cycle length in a second pass.
solution_variants.complexity: O(m) queries with a small constant factor

tags.evidence: The correctness relies on the unique-cycle structure of a cactus and the fact that deleting two edges on the same cycle disconnects it.
tags.description: Graph problems whose key structure is that every edge belongs to at most one simple cycle.
tags.created_reason: Cactus graph structure is a standard graph class and a useful retrieval target beyond generic graph tags.

tags.evidence: The algorithm is designed around connectivity queries to an interactor.
Each edge's bracket is found by binary searching a monotone predicate over 16 possible brackets.
One classification outcome is whether deleting a single edge disconnects the graph, i.e. whether it is a bridge.
The connectivity predicate Ask(G_i \ {e}) changes monotonically across bracket index i.
